channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill 25 people in Gaza as Supreme Court hears Shin Bet cases
item.description: Palestinian medics say Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip overnight and into Tuesday have killed at least 25 people, including eight children and five women.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Contract for Hong Kong company to operate Panama Canal ports had irregularities, audit finds
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panama’s comptroller authority said Monday an audit found irregularities in the renewal of a 25-year port concession in the interoceanic canal and it would request an investigation into the authorization of the contract to a Hong Kong company.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Prince Harry appeals the loss of his UK security detail
item.description: Prince Harry arrived at a London appeals court to challenge the U.K. government's decision to strip him of his publicly funded security detail.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea will hold a presidential election June 3 to choose Yoon's successor
item.description: South Korea will hold a snap presidential election June 3 to choose Yoon Suk Yeol’s successor after the conservative was ousted over his imposition of martial law late last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Altar found in Guatemalan jungle evidence of mingling of Mayan and Teotihuacan cultures, experts say
item.description: An altar from the Teotihuacan culture, at the pre-Hispanic heart of what became Mexico, has been discovered in Tikal National Park in Guatemala, the center of Mayan culture, demonstrating the interaction between the two societies.
item.dc:creator: Sonia Pérez D., Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Americans deeply divided on tariffs, foreign alliances: Poll
item.description: Americans are deeply divided along partisan lines about tariffs and the U.S.'s foreign alliances, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rescue efforts from Myanmar's deadly earthquake wind down as death toll hits 3,600
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Long-shot efforts to find survivors from Myanmar’s devastating March 28 earthquake were winding down Monday, as rescue efforts were supplanted by increasing relief and recovery activity. The death toll surpassed 3,600 and was still climbing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump: US, Iran to hold direct nuclear talks on Saturday
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. will hold direct talks with Iran over its nuclear program, saying a meeting is set for Saturday. He made his remarks alongside Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in a meeting that was arranged on short notice.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu nix White House press conference
item.description: The White House on Monday opted to nix a formal press conference that would have had President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu taking questions from a wider swath of reporters, choosing instead for the two leaders to face the small group of media in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: King Charles III arrives in Rome on his first overseas trip since a brief hospitalization
item.description: ROME (AP) — King Charles III arrived in Rome on Monday for a state visit to Italy on his first overseas trip since being briefly hospitalized for side effects of cancer treatment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani security forces kill 9 militants in a raid near the Afghan border, the military says
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani security forces raided a militant hideout in the restive northwest near the Afghan border, killing nine militants, the military said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7 GOP senators sign on to bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators, including Sen. Chuck Grassley (Iowa), the Senate’s president pro tempore, and Sen. Mitch McConnell (Ky.), the former Senate Republican leader, have signed on to a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Trump: Trade deficit with China must be resolved before deal on tariffs
item.description: President Trump said the trade deficit between the U.S. and China must be resolved before he is willing to negotiate a deal the regarding tariffs he placed on Beijing. “When you look at the trade deficit we have with certain countries … with China, it’s a trillion dollars.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Thousands of Ukrainian civilians are still held by Russia with uncertain hope of release
item.description: When she heard her front door open almost two years ago, Kostiantyn Zinovkin’s mother thought her son had returned home because he forgot something. Instead, men in balaclavas burst into the apartment in Melitopol, a southern Ukrainian city occupied by Russian forces.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian air strikes kill 1 in Kyiv as Zelenskyy demands more pressure on Putin
item.description: One person was killed in Russian air strikes on Kyiv on Sunday while Kryvyi Rih mourns 19 victims of Friday's attack, which local authorities say also destroyed dozens of homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel walks back its account of the killing of 15 medics in Gaza after video seems to contradict it
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Israeli military backtracked on its account of the killing of 15 Palestinian medics by its forces last month after phone video appeared to contradict its claims that their vehicles did not have emergency signals on when troops opened fire on them in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, WAFAA SHURAFA and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: The Who... not too old to rock
item.description: Roger Daltrey and Pete Townshend lit up London in a rare, fiery performance for Teenage Cancer Trust — decades older, still unmistakably The Who.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy meets European military leaders to plan for a peacekeeping force
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met the leaders of the British and French armed forces in Kyiv Saturday to discuss the potential deployment of a multinational peacekeeping force to Ukraine, despite the reluctance of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentina's Milei doubles down on Trump bromance as the world reels from trade shock
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Europe vowed retaliation. China plotted tariffs of its own. Mexico scrambled to blunt the blow. But while the world's leaders were wringing their hands over President Donald Trump's announcement of sweeping tariffs on U.S.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cambodia's leader presides at ceremony for upgrade of naval base with help from China
item.description: SIHANOUKVILLE, Cambodia (AP) — Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet on Saturday presided over the opening of an expansion of his country’s main naval base, which analysts and the U.S. government suspect will be used as a strategic outpost by China.
item.dc:creator: SOPHENG CHEANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urged to review UN immunity, lax visa rules amid national security concerns
item.description: As the U.S. weighs U.N. funding cuts, experts urge the Trump administration to review the world body's visa process and staff immunity over continued security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
